data: Strategic Alliances and Foreign Ownership Exceptions

Malaysia granted Starlink approval to operate as a 100% foreign-owned entity in July 2023, an exception to the standard 49% foreign equity cap for license holders — following a direct video call between PM Anwar Ibrahim and Elon Musk.
3 providers authorized · 98% internet penetration · 100% foreign ownership granted to Starlink
100% foreign ownership granted to Starlink — the only company exempted from Malaysia's 49% equity cap for telecoms
This decision followed a video call between Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim and Elon Musk where both agreed to strengthen the investment relationship.
Malaysia granted Starlink a rare exception to operate as a fully foreign-owned entity despite the standard 49% foreign equity cap.
The local incumbent MEASAT has formed alliances with Chinese LEO firms like Shanghai Spacesail to explore satellite-based IoT.
The country lacks a unified framework integrating privacy, cybersecurity, and competition, leading to an asymmetry between public and private sector accountability.

Malaysia presents the starkest case of regulatory exception among the studied countries. Starlink was licensed in July 2023 following a direct video call between Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim and Elon Musk — and was granted 100% foreign ownership, bypassing the standard 49% equity cap applied to all other foreign telecoms operators. With 98% internet penetration, Malaysia's headline connectivity figures are strong. Yet only 46.7% of households have fixed broadband, and the complete absence of a unified framework for privacy, cybersecurity, and cross-border data governance leaves the country structurally exposed. Local player Angkasa-X launched its first LEO satellite in June 2023, but remains operationally limited. The government has simultaneously ordered 40 Starlink devices for immediate deployment in schools and universities.
Licensing followed a direct video call between PM Anwar Ibrahim and Elon Musk; a 100% foreign ownership exception was granted, versus the standard 49% equity cap.
1 of 7 governance indicators present, and it is a negative exception rather than a protection: a 100% foreign ownership exception was granted, unique among studied countries.
100% foreign ownership exception granted to Starlink — the only company exempted from Malaysia's standard 49% equity cap
Licensing followed a direct PM video call with Elon Musk — no public consultation or competitive process
No unified framework for privacy, cybersecurity, or cross-border data governance
46.7% fixed broadband penetration despite 98% internet use — structural dependency on mobile/satellite
Enforcement of equity cap parity for all foreign telecoms operators
Unified data governance and cybersecurity framework

Who controls satellite internet? We’re tracking who profits from connectivity in space and who gets left behind.
